channel.title: E2E testing of the CLI
channel.description:
# Description We want to create E2E test that cover the whole lifecycle of the Frontity CLI. Those tests should be able to: - launch the CLI on diferent platforms (Win, MacOS) (optional?) - launch the CLI with any arguments - pass interactive arguments to the CLI - assert that particular files have been created - check that a frontity server has been launched and returns pages (with cypress?)
# Existing frameworks - https://github.com/emilkarlen/exactly - https://github.com/rylandg/shrun

item.title: E2E testing of the CLI
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
I’m changing this to “Released”. Thanks a lot @mmczaplinski

Read full topic

item.title: E2E testing of the CLI
item.dc:creator: mmczaplinski
item.description:
This has been implemented in:

github.com/frontity/frontity

Create a git repo when running `frontity create` and add a `--no-git` flag to opt out of this behaviour

frontity:dev ← frontity:cli-git-option
opened 04:52AM - 15 Apr 21 UTC
orballo
+7387 -4466

**What**: Automatically initialize a fresh repository when when running `fron…tity create`. Also add a `--no-git` flag which opts out of creating the repo. **Why**: Because it is annoying to create the git repo manually after creating a project. **How**: Creates a `.gitignore` file and runs `git init`, `git add .` and `git commit -m "Initialized with Frontity"`. **Tasks**: - [x] Code - [x] TSDocs - [x] TypeScript - [x] Unit tests - [x] Update community discussions - [x] Add a changeset (with link to its [Feature Discussion](https://community.frontity.org/c/33) if it exists) - [x] End to end tests - [x] Add README for new cli-e2e tests **Unrelated Tasks** - [ ] TypeScript tests - [ ] Update starter themes - [ ] Update other packages **Additional Comments**

For more details, you can check out the packages/frontity/e2e-cli/README.md.

Features supported

mmczaplinski:
  • launch the CLI on diferent platforms (Win, MacOS) (optional?)

Yes. This is possible by running the tests on github actions which support Win, Linux and MacOS containers.

mmczaplinski:
  • launch the CLI with any arguments

Yes. Indeed you can launch the CLI with any arguments, the testing “framework”, if you can call it that, provides a helper to run an arbitrary command inside an isolated container.

mmczaplinski:
  • pass interactive arguments to the CLI

Sort of. For the moment this is not supported out of the box, but since the tests are built on top of execa, it could be accomplished with something like:

const execa = require("execa");
const { Readable } = require("stream");

const subprocess = execa("frontity create --theme @frontity/mars-theme");

// The name of the project that is gonna be passed interactively 
const readable = Readable.from(["my-awesome-project"]);

// Pass it to std input
readable.pipe(subprocess.stdin);

// Actually run the `create` command 
const { stdout } = await subprocess;

console.log(stdout);
mmczaplinski:
  • assert that particular files have been created

Yes. The framework helpers can run arbitrary unix commands inside the container like ls, cat or tree and capture their output.

mmczaplinski:
  • check that a frontity server has been launched and returns pages (with cypress?)

Sort of. At the moment there is no built-in way to do this, but helpers can be created in a similar way as those for passing the interactive CLI arguments.

Read full topic

item.title: E2E testing of the CLI
item.dc:creator: mmczaplinski
item.description:
Description

We want to create E2E test that cover the whole lifecycle of the Frontity CLI. Those tests should be able to:

  • launch the CLI on diferent platforms (Win, MacOS) (optional?)
  • launch the CLI with any arguments
  • pass interactive arguments to the CLI
  • assert that particular files have been created
  • check that a frontity server has been launched and returns pages (with cypress?)

Existing frameworks

  • GitHub - emilkarlen/exactly: Exactly - tests a command line program by executing it in a temporary sandbox directory and inspecting its result.
  • GitHub - rylandg/shrun: Test CLI commands in isolated containers

Read full topic
